patch.value: Isolate public package verification from publish authentication

The tag workflow published successfully, but its public npm/npx/Bun verifier inherited setup-node's authenticated npm client configuration and exhausted retries even though the exact package was publicly usable.

Publishing continues to use the protected npm token and provenance.; Post-publish npm, npx, and Bun checks run with a clean public registry configuration.; An idempotent existing-tag dispatch completes exact-version verification and creates the GitHub Release.
Prevents publish credentials from affecting anonymous package verification.
Authentication is scoped to publishing; verification proves public usability.
The first successful package publish exposed the Actions-only client configuration leak.
Publish with setup-node registry authentication, then run the public verifier in a later step without replacing the generated npm user configuration.
The exact public version resolves through npm metadata, npx, and Bun.
Publishing succeeded, but the verifier exhausted its retry window in the inherited authenticated client context.
The package was available, but the release workflow and GitHub Release remained incomplete.
npm metadata and Bun resolved the public package, but npx selected the current checkout and could not find its self-installed jf binary, exhausting retries.
From a jellyfin-cli checkout whose package version equals the requested registry version, run npx with --package jellyfin-cli@that-version and invoke jf.

The tag workflow published successfully, but public verification ran inside the same-name, same-version release checkout. npm treated that checkout as the requested package even though its own bin was not self-installed; publish-oriented client state also remained in scope.
Isolate public package verification from release checkout and authentication

value.note: Public registry and isolated working-directory verification boundary

author: codex-release-agent
message: Link public and full release proofs

author: codex-release-agent

value.author: codex-release-agent
value.text: Root-cause proof: inside the same-name, same-version release checkout, npm metadata and Bun succeeded while npx selected the checkout and could not find a self-installed jf binary. After moving all consumer checks to an empty temporary working directory and clearing publish authentication, exact 2026.7.21 verification passed npm, npx, and Bun on attempt one.

message: Record sanitized root-cause and fix proof

patch.value: Exact public 2026.7.21 verification passed npm metadata, npx, and Bun on the first attempt; the complete local release gate passed with 895 tests, 177 credential-dependent skips, and zero failures.
Separated publishing from public verification, cleared publish client state, and ran registry consumers from an empty temporary directory so npm cannot select the same-version release checkout.
Fixed public verification by isolating both registry configuration and working directory from the authenticated same-version release checkout; exact npm/npx/Bun and full release gates passed.
